Text:

Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a Diminuto or larger creature touches or enters the warded area. When you cast the spell, you can designate creatures that won't set off the alarm. You also choose whether the alarm is mental or audible.

A mental alarm alerts you with a ping in your mind if you are within 1 mile of the warded area. This ping awakens you if you are sleeping.

An audible alarm produces the sound of a hand bell for 10 seconds within 60 pies.

Name: Alter Self
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Moon (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer
Text:
You assume a different form. When you cast the spell, choose one of the following options, the effects of which last for the Duracion of the spell. While the spell lasts, you can end one option as an action to gain the benefits of a different one.

Aquatic Adaptation. You adapt your body to an aquatic environment, sprouting gills and growing webbing between your fingers. You can breathe underwater and gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

Change Appearance. You transform your appearance. You decide what you look like, including your height, weight, facial features, sound of your voice, hair length, coloration, and distinguishing characteristics, if any. You can make yourself appear as a member of another race, though none of your statistics change. You also can't appear as a creature of a different size than you, and your basic shape stays the same; if you're bipedal, you can't use this spell to become quadrupedal, for instance. At any time for the Duracion of the spell, you can use your action to change your appearance in this way again.

Natural Weapons. You grow claws, fangs, spines, horns, or a different natural arma of your choice. Your unarmed strikes deal 1d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing dano, as appropriate to the natural arma you chose, and you are proficient with your unarmed strikes. Finally, the natural arma is magic and you have a +1 bonus to the attack and dano rolls you make using it.

Name: Animal Messenger
Components: V, S, M (a morsel of food)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
By means of this spell, you use an animal to deliver a message. Choose a Diminuto Bestia you can see within Alcance, such as a squirrel, a blue jay, or a bat. You specify a location, which you must have visited, and a recipient who matches a general description, such as "a man or woman dressed in the uniform of the town guard" or "a red-haired dwarf wearing a pointed hat." You also speak a message of up to twenty-five words. el objetivo Bestia travels for the Duracion of the spell toward the specified location, covering about 50 miles per 24 hours for a flying messenger, or 25 miles for other animals.

When the messenger arrives, it delivers your message to the creature that you described, replicating the sound of your voice. The messenger speaks only to a creature matching the description you gave. If the messenger doesn't reach its destination before the spell ends, the message is lost, and the Bestia makes its way back to where you cast this spell.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. If you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the Duracion of the spell increases by 48 hours for each slot level above 2nd.

You also communicate a message of up to twenty-five words. The Bestia travels for the Duracion toward the specified location, covering about 25 miles per 24 hours or 50 miles if the Bestia can Volar.

When the Bestia arrives, it delivers your message to the creature that you described, mimicking your communication. If the Bestia doesn't reach its destination before the spell ends, the message is lost, and the Bestia returns to where you cast the spell.

Name: Animal Shapes
Text:
Your magic turns others into beasts. Choose any number of willing creatures that you can see within Alcance. You transform each target into the form of a Grande or smaller Bestia with a challenge rating of 4 or lower. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to transform affected creatures into new forms.

The transformation lasts for the Duracion for each target, or until el objetivo drops to 0 puntos de golpe or dies. You can choose a different form for each target. A target's game statistics are replaced by the statistics of the chosen Bestia, though el objetivo retains its alignment and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. el objetivo assumes the puntos de golpe of its new form, and when it reverts to its normal form, it returns to the number of puntos de golpe it had before it transformed. If it reverts as a result of dropping to 0 puntos de golpe, any excess dano carries over to its normal form. As long as the excess dano doesn't reduce the creature's normal form to 0 puntos de golpe, it isn't knocked inconsciente. The creature is limited in the actions it can perform by the nature of its new form, and it can't speak or cast spells.

el objetivo's gear melds into the new form. el objetivo can't activate, wield, or otherwise benefit from any of its equipment.

Name: Animal Shapes
Text:
Choose any number of willing creatures that you can see within Alcance. Each target shape-shifts into a Grande or smaller Bestia of your choice that has a Challenge Rating of 4 or lower. You can choose a different form for each target. On later turns, you can take a Magic action to transform the targets again.

A target's game statistics are replaced by the chosen Bestia's statistics, but el objetivo retains its creature type; puntos de golpe; Hit Point Dice; alignment; ability to communicate; and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. el objetivo's actions are limited by the Bestia form's anatomy, and it can't cast spells. el objetivo's equipment melds into the new form, and el objetivo can't use any of that equipment while in that form.

el objetivo gains a number of Temporary puntos de golpe equal to the puntos de golpe of the first form into which it shape-shifts. These Temporary puntos de golpe vanish if any remain when the spell ends. The transformation lasts for the Duracion or until el objetivo ends it as a Bonus Action.

Name: Animate Dead
Components: V, S, M (a drop of blood, a piece of flesh, and a pinch of bone dust)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Spores (TCE) Druid, Spores (TCE) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin
Text:
This spell creates an No muerto servant. Choose a pile of bones or a corpse of a Mediano or Pequeno Humanoide within Alcance. Your spell imbues el objetivo with a foul mimicry of life, raising it as an No muerto creature. el objetivo becomes a skeleton if you chose bones or a zombie if you chose a corpse (the DM has the creature's game statistics).

On each of your turns, you can use a bonus action to mentally command any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 60 pies of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the creature only defends itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.

The creature is under your control for 24 hours, after which it stops obeying any command you've given it. To maintain control of the creature for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell on the creature again before the current 24-hour period ends. This use of the spell reasserts your control over up to four creatures you have animated with this spell, rather than animating a new one.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you animate or reassert control over two additional No muerto creatures for each slot level above 3rd. Each of the creatures must come from a different corpse or pile of bones.

Subclasses: Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric
Text:

Subclasses: Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, No muerto (VRGR) Warlock, No muerto (VRGR) Warlock
Text:

The barrier prevents an affected creature from passing or reaching through. An affected creature can cast spells or make attacks with ranged or reach weapons through the barrier.

Name: Antimagic Field
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of powdered iron or iron filings)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
A 10-foot-radius invisible sphere of antimagic surrounds you. This area is divorced from the magical energy that suffuses the multiverse. Within the sphere, spells can't be cast, summoned creatures disappear, and even magic items become mundane. Until the spell ends, the sphere moves with you, centered on you.

Spells and other magical effects, except those created by an artifact or a deity, are suppressed in the sphere and can't protrude into it. A slot expended to cast a suppressed spell is consumed. While an effect is suppressed, it doesn't function, but the time it spends suppressed counts against its Duracion.

Targeted Effects. Spells and other magical effects, such as magic missile and charm person, that target a creature or an object in the sphere have no effect on that target.

Areas of Magic. The area of another spell or magical effect, such as fireball, can't extend into the sphere. If the sphere overlaps an area of magic, the part of the area that is covered by the sphere is suppressed. For example, the flames created by a wall of fire are suppressed within the sphere, creating a gap in the wall if the overlap is Grande enough.

Spells. Any active spell or other magical effect on a creature or an object in the sphere is suppressed while the creature or object is in it.

Magic Items. The properties and powers of magic items are suppressed in the sphere. For example, a +1 longsword in the sphere functions as a nonmagical longsword.

A magic arma's properties and powers are suppressed if it is used against a target in the sphere or wielded by an attacker in the sphere. If a magic arma or a piece of magic ammunition fully leaves the sphere (for example, if you fire a magic arrow or throw a magic spear at a target outside the sphere), the magic of the item ceases to be suppressed as soon as it exits.

Magical Travel. Teleportation and planar travel fail to work in the sphere, whether the sphere is the destination or the departure point for such magical travel. A portal to another location, world, or plane of existence, as well as an opening to an extradimensional space such as that created by the rope trick spell, temporarily closes while in the sphere.

Creatures and Objects. A creature or object summoned or created by magic temporarily winks out of existence in the sphere. Such a creature instantly reappears once the space the creature occupied is no longer within the sphere.

Dispel Magic. Spells and magical effects such as dispel magic have no effect on the sphere. Likewise, the spheres created by different antimagic field spells don't nullify each other.

Name: Antimagic Field
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
An aura of antimagic surrounds you in a 10-foot Emanation. No one can cast spells, take Magic actions, or create other magical effects inside the aura, and those things can't target or otherwise affect anything inside it. Magical properties of magic items don't work inside the aura or on anything inside it.

Areas of effect created by spells or other magic can't extend into the aura, and no one can teleport into or out of it or use planar travel there. Portals close temporarily while in the aura.

Ongoing spells, except those cast by an Artifact or a deity, are suppressed in the area. While an effect is suppressed, it doesn't function, but the time it spends suppressed counts against its Duracion.

Dispel Magic has no effect on the aura, and the auras created by different Antimagic Field spells don't nullify each other.

Name: Antipathy/Sympathy
Components: V, S, M (either a lump of alum soaked in vinegar for the antipathy effect or a drop of honey for the sympathy effect)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
This spell attracts or repels creatures of your choice. You target something within Alcance, either a Enorme or smaller object or creature or an area that is no larger than a 200-foot cube. Then specify a kind of intelligent creature, such as red dragons, goblins, or vampires. You invest el objetivo with an aura that either attracts or repels the specified creatures for the Duracion. Choose antipathy or sympathy as the aura's effect.

Antipathy. The enchantment causes creatures of the kind you designated to feel an intense urge to leave the area and avoid el objetivo. When such a creature can see el objetivo or comes within 60 pies of it, the creature debe superar a Wisdom tirada de salvacion or become asustado. The creature remains asustado while it can see el objetivo or is within 60 pies of it. While asustado by el objetivo, the creature must use its movement to move to the nearest safe spot from which it can't see el objetivo. If the creature moves more than 60 pies from el objetivo and can't see it, the creature is no longer asustado, but the creature becomes asustado again if it regains sight of el objetivo or moves within 60 pies of it.

Sympathy. The enchantment causes the specified creatures to feel an intense urge to approach el objetivo while within 60 pies of it or able to see it. When such a creature can see el objetivo or comes within 60 pies of it, the creature debe superar a Wisdom tirada de salvacion or use its movement on each of its turns to enter the area or move within reach of el objetivo. When the creature has done so, it can't willingly move away from el objetivo.

If el objetivo damages or otherwise harms an affected creature, the affected creature can make a Wisdom tirada de salvacion to end the effect, as described below.

Ending the Effect. If an affected creature ends its turn while not within 60 pies of el objetivo or able to see it, the creature makes a Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a successful save, the creature is no longer affected by el objetivo and recognizes the feeling of repugnance or attraction as magical. In addition, a creature affected by the spell is allowed another Wisdom tirada de salvacion every 24 hours while the spell persists.

A creature that successfully saves against this effect is immune to it for 1 minute, after which time it can be affected again.

Name: Antipathy/Sympathy
Components: V, S, M (a mix of vinegar and honey)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
As you cast the spell, choose whether it creates antipathy or sympathy, and target una criatura or object that is Enorme or smaller. Then specify a kind of creature, such as red dragons, goblins, or vampires. A creature of the chosen kind makes a Wisdom tirada de salvacion when it comes within 120 pies of el objetivo. Your choice of antipathy or sympathy determines what happens to a creature when it fails that save:

Antipathy. The creature has the asustado condition. The asustado creature must use its movement on its turns to get as far away as possible from el objetivo, moving by the safest route.

Sympathy. The creature has the hechizado condition. The hechizado creature must use its movement on its turns to get as close as possible to el objetivo, moving by the safest route. If the creature is within 5 pies of el objetivo, the creature can't willingly move away. If el objetivo damages the hechizado creature, that creature can make a Wisdom tirada de salvacion to end the effect, as described below.

Ending the Effect. If the asustado or hechizado creature ends its turn more than 120 pies away from el objetivo, the creature makes a Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a successful save, the creature is no longer affected by el objetivo. A creature that successfully saves against this effect is immune to it for 1 minute, after which it can be affected again.

Any creature or object entering the portal exits from the other portal as if the two were adjacent to each other; passing through a portal from the nonportal side has no effect. Name: Astral Projection
Components: V, S, M (for cada criatura you affect with this spell, you must provide one jacinth worth at least 1,000 gp and one ornately carved bar of silver worth at least 100 gp, all of which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You and up to eight willing creatures within Alcance project your astral bodies into the Astral Plane (the spell fails and the casting is wasted if you are already on that plane). The material body you leave behind is inconsciente and in a state of suspended animation, it doesn't need food or air and doesn't age.

Your astral body resembles your mortal form in almost every way, replicating your game statistics and possessions. The principal difference is the addition of a silvery cord that extends from between your shoulder blades and trails behind you, fading to invisibility after 1 foot. This cord is your tether to your material body. As long as the tether remains intact, you can find your way home. If the cord is cut-something that can happen only when an effect specifically states that it does-your soul and body are separated, killing you instantly.

Your astral form can freely travel through the Astral Plane and can pass through portals there leading to any other plane. If you enter a new plane or return to the plane you were on when casting this spell, your body and possessions are transported along the silver cord, allowing you to re-enter your body as you enter the new plane. Your astral form is a separate incarnation. Any dano or other effects that apply to it have no effect on your physical body, nor do they persist when you return to it.

The spell ends for you and your companions when you use your action to dismiss it. When the spell ends, the affected creature returns to its physical body, and it awakens.

The spell might also end early for you or one of your companions. A successful dispel magic spell used against an astral or physical body ends the spell for that creature. If a creature's original body or its astral form drops to 0 puntos de golpe, the spell ends for that creature. If the spell ends and the silver cord is intact, the cord pulls the creature's astral form back to its body, ending its state of suspended animation.

If you are returned to your body prematurely, your companions remain in their astral forms and must find their own way back to their bodies, usually by dropping to 0 puntos de golpe.

When the spell ends for a target who isn't dead, el objetivo reappears in its body and exits the state of suspended animation.

Name: Bigby's Hand
Components: V, S, M (an eggshell and a snakeskin glove)
Subclasses: Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You create a Grande hand of shimmering, translucent force in an unoccupied space that you can see within Alcance. The hand lasts for the spell's Duracion, and it moves at your command, mimicking the movements of your own hand.

The hand is an object that has AC 20 and puntos de golpe equal to your hit point maximum. If it drops to 0 puntos de golpe, the spell ends. It has a Strength of 26 (+8) and a Dexterity of 10 (+0). The hand doesn't fill its space.

When you cast the spell and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns, you can move the hand up to 60 pies and then cause one of the following effects with it.

Clenched Fist. The hand strikes una criatura or object within 5 pies of it. Make a melee spell attack for the hand using your game statistics. On a hit, el objetivo recibe 4d8 force dano.

Forceful Hand. The hand attempts to push a creature within 5 pies of it in a direction you choose. Make a check with the hand's Strength contested by the Strength (Athletics) check of el objetivo. If el objetivo is Mediano or smaller, you have advantage on the check. If you succeed, the hand pushes el objetivo up to 5 pies plus a number of pies equal to five times your spellcasting ability modifier. The hand moves with el objetivo to remain within 5 pies of it.

Grasping Hand. The hand attempts to grapple a Enorme or smaller creature within 5 pies of it. You use the hand's Strength score to resolve the grapple. If el objetivo is Mediano or smaller, you have advantage on the check. While the hand is grappling el objetivo, you can use a bonus action to have the hand crush it. When you do so, el objetivo recibe bludgeoning dano equal to 2d6 + your spellcasting ability modifier.

Interposing Hand. The hand interposes itself between you and a creature you choose until you give the hand a different command. The hand moves to stay between you and el objetivo, providing you with half cover against el objetivo. el objetivo can't move through the hand's space if its Strength score is less than or equal to the hand's Strength score. If its Strength score is higher than the hand's Strength score, el objetivo can move toward you through the hand's space, but that space is difficult terrain for el objetivo.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the dano from the clenched fist option increases by 2d8 and the dano from the grasping hand increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 5th.

Name: Clone
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth at least 1,000 gp and at least 1 cubic inch of flesh of the creature that is to be cloned, which the spell consumes, and a vessel worth at least 2,000 gp that has a sealable lid and is Grande enough to hold the creature being cloned, such as a Enorme urn, coffin, mud-filled cyst in the ground, or crystal container filled with salt water)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
This spell grows an inert duplicate of a living creature as a safeguard against death. This clone forms inside the vessel used in the spell's casting and grows to full size and maturity after 120 days; you can also choose to have the clone be a younger version of the same creature. It remains inert and endures indefinitely, as long as its vessel remains undisturbed.

At any time after the clone matures, if the original creature dies, its soul transfers to the clone, provided that the soul is free and willing to return. The clone is physically identical to the original and has the same personality, memories, and abilities, but none of the original's equipment. The original creature's physical remains, if they still exist, become inert and can't thereafter be restored to life, since the creature's soul is elsewhere.

Name: Clone
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes, and a sealable vessel worth 2,000+ GP that is Grande enough to hold the creature being cloned)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You touch a creature or at least 1 cubic inch of its flesh. An inert duplicate of that creature forms inside the vessel used in the spell's casting and finishes growing after 120 days; you choose whether the finished clone is the same age as the creature or younger. The clone remains inert and endures indefinitely while its vessel remains undisturbed.

If the original creature dies after the clone finishes forming, the creature's soul transfers to the clone if the soul is free and willing to return. The clone is physically identical to the original and has the same personality, memories, and abilities, but none of the original's equipment. The creature's original remains, if any, become inert and can't be revived, since the creature's soul is elsewhere.

Approach. el objetivo moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 pies of you.

Drop. el objetivo drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

Flee. el objetivo spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can affect one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 pies of each other when you target them.

You contact your deity or a divine proxy and ask up to three questions that can be answered with a yes or no. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. You receive a correct answer for each question.

Divine beings aren't necessarily omniscient, so you might receive "unclear" as an answer if a question pertains to information that lies beyond the deity's knowledge. In a case where a one-word answer could be misleading or contrary to the deity's interests, the DM might offer a short phrase as an answer instead.

If you cast the spell two or more times before finishing your next descanso largo, there is a cumulative 25 percent chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer. The DM makes this roll in secret.

A creature can Nadar away from the vortex by making a Strength (Athletics) check against your CD de salvacion de conjuros.

When a creature enters the vortex for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Strength tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature recibe 2d8 bludgeoning dano and is caught in the vortex until the spell ends. On a successful save, the creature recibe half dano, and isn't caught in the vortex. A creature caught in the vortex can use its action to try to Nadar away from the vortex as described above, but has disadvantage on the Strength (Athletics) check to do so.

The first time each turn that an object enters the vortex, the object recibe 2d8 bludgeoning dano; this dano occurs each round it remains in the vortex.

Name: Control Water
Components: V, S, M (a mixture of water and dust)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
Until the spell ends, you control any water inside an area you choose that is a Cube up to 100 pies on a side, using one of the following effects. As a Magic action on your later turns, you can repeat the same effect or choose a different one.

Flood. You cause the water level of all standing water in the area to rise by as much as 20 pies. If you choose an area in a Grande body of water, you instead create a 20-foot tall wave that travels from one side of the area to the other and then crashes. Any Enorme or smaller vehicles in the wave's path are carried with it to the other side. Any Enorme or smaller vehicles struck by the wave have a 25 percent chance of capsizing.

The water level remains elevated until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. If this effect produced a wave, the wave repeats on the start of your next turn while the flood effect lasts.

Part Water. You part water in the area and create a trench. The trench extends across the spell's area, and the separated water forms a wall to either side. The trench remains until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. The water then slowly fills in the trench over the course of the next round until the normal water level is restored.

Redirect Flow. You cause flowing water in the area to move in a direction you choose, even if the water has to flow over obstacles, up walls, or in other unlikely directions. The water in the area moves as you direct it, but once it moves beyond the spell's area, it resumes its flow based on the terrain. The water continues to move in the direction you chose until the spell ends or you choose a different effect.

Whirlpool. You cause a whirlpool to form in the center of the area, which must be at least 50 pies square and 25 pies deep. The whirlpool lasts until you choose a different effect or the spell ends. The whirlpool is 5 pies wide at the base, up to 50 pies wide at the top, and 25 pies tall. Any creature in the water and within 25 pies of the whirlpool is pulled 10 pies toward it. When a creature enters the whirlpool for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it makes a Strength tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature recibe 2d8 Bludgeoning dano. On a successful save, the creature recibe half as much dano. A creature can Nadar away from the whirlpool only if it first recibe an action to pull away and succeeds on a Strength (Athletics) check against your CD de salvacion de conjuros.

Name: Control Weather
Components: V, S, M (burning incense and bits of earth and wood mixed in water)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You take control of the weather within 5 miles of you for the Duracion. You must be outdoors to cast this spell. Moving to a place where you don't have a clear path to the sky ends the spell early.

When you cast the spell, you change the current weather conditions, which are determined by the DM based on the climate and season. You can change precipitation, temperature, and wind. It recibe 1d4 × 10 minutes for the new conditions to take effect. Once they do so, you can change the conditions again. When the spell ends, the weather gradually returns to normal.

When you change the weather conditions, find a current condition on the following tables and change its stage by one, up or down. When changing the wind, you can change its direction.

PrecipitationStageCondition1Clear2Light clouds3Overcast or ground fog4Rain, hail, or snow5Torrential rain, driving hail, or blizzardTemperatureStageCondition1Unbearable heat2Hot3Warm4Cool5Cold6Arctic coldWindStageCondition1Calm2Moderate wind3Strong wind4Gale5Storm

Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You take control of the weather within 5 miles of you for the Duracion. You must be outdoors to cast this spell, and it ends early if you go indoors.

When you cast the spell, you change the current weather conditions, which are determined by the DM. You can change precipitation, temperature, and wind. It recibe 1d4 × 10 minutes for the new conditions to take effect. Once they do so, you can change the conditions again. When the spell ends, the weather gradually returns to normal.

When you change the weather conditions, find a current condition on the following tables and change its stage by one, up or down. When changing the wind, you can change its direction.
