Name: Wall of Ice
Components: V, S, M (a piece of quartz)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create a wall of ice on a solid surface within Alcance. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a globe with a radius of up to 10 pies, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-square panels. Each panel must be contiguous with another panel. In any form, the wall is 1 foot thick and lasts for the Duracion.

If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side) and makes a Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 10d6 Cold dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

The wall is an object that can be damaged and thus breached. It has AC 12 and 30 puntos de golpe per 10-foot section, and it has Immunity to Cold, Poison, and Psychic dano and Vulnerability to Fire dano. Reducing a 10-foot section of wall to 0 puntos de golpe destroys it and leaves behind a sheet of frigid air in the space the wall occupied.

A creature moving through the sheet of frigid air for the first time on a turn makes a Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 5d6 Cold dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The dano the wall deals when it appears increases by 2d6 and the dano from passing through the sheet of frigid air increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Wall of Light
Optional/Variant Classes: Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Warlock (PHB'14), Warlock (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A shimmering wall of bright light appears at a point you choose within Alcance. The wall appears in any orientation you choose: horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. It can be free floating, or it can rest on a solid surface. The wall can be up to 60 pies long, 10 pies high, and 5 pies thick. The wall blocks line of sight, but creatures and objects can pass through it. It emits bright light out to 120 pies and dim light for an additional 120 pies.

When the wall appears, cada criatura in its area must make a Constitution tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a creature recibe 4d8 radiant dano, and it is cegado for 1 minute. On a successful save, it recibe half as much dano and isn't cegado. A cegado creature can make a Constitution tirada de salvacion at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

A creature that ends its turn in the wall's area recibe 4d8 radiant dano.

Until the spell ends, you can use an action to launch a beam of radiance from the wall at una criatura you can see within 60 pies of it. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, el objetivo recibe 4d8 radiant dano. Whether you hit or miss, reduce the length of the wall by 10 pies. If the wall's length drops to 0 pies, the spell ends.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the dano increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.

Name: Wall of Sand
Components: V, S, M (a handful of sand)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: You create a wall of swirling sand on the ground at a point you can see within Alcance. You can make the wall up to 30 pies long, 10 pies high, and 10 pies thick, and it vanishes when the spell ends. It blocks line of sight but not movement. A creature is cegado while in the wall's space and must spend 3 pies of movement for every 1 foot it moves there.

Name: Wall of Stone
Components: V, S, M (a Pequeno block of granite)
Subclasses: Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
A nonmagical wall of solid stone springs into existence at a point you choose within Alcance. The wall is 6 inches thick and is composed of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. Each panel must be contiguous with at least one other panel. Alternatively, you can create 10-foot-by-20-foot panels that are only 3 inches thick.

If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (your choice). If a creature would be surrounded on all sides by the wall (or the wall and another solid surface), that creature can make a Dexterity tirada de salvacion. On a success, it can use its reaction to move up to its speed so that it is no longer enclosed by the wall.

The wall can have any shape you desire, though it can't occupy the same space as a creature or object. The wall doesn't need to be vertical or rest on any firm foundation. It must, however, merge with and be solidly supported by existing stone. Thus, you can use this spell to bridge a chasm or create a ramp.

If you create a span greater than 20 pies in length, you must halve the size of each panel to create supports. You can crudely shape the wall to create crenellations, battlements, and so on.

The wall is an object made of stone that can be damaged and thus breached. Each panel has AC 15 and 30 puntos de golpe per inch of thickness. Reducing a panel to 0 puntos de golpe destroys it and might cause connected panels to collapse at the DM's discretion.

If you maintain your concentration on this spell for its whole Duracion, the wall becomes permanent and can't be dispelled. Otherwise, the wall disappears when the spell ends.

Name: Wall of Thorns
Components: V, S, M (a handful of thorns)
Text:
You create a wall of tough, pliable, tangled brush bristling with needle-sharp thorns. The wall appears within Alcance on a solid surface and lasts for the Duracion. You choose to make the wall up to 60 pies long, 10 pies high, and 5 pies thick or a circle that has a 20-foot diameter and is up to 20 pies high and 5 pies thick. The wall blocks line of sight.

When the wall appears, cada criatura within its area must make a Dexterity tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a creature recibe 7d8 piercing dano, or half as much dano on a successful save.

A creature can move through the wall, albeit slowly and painfully. For every 1 foot a creature moves through the wall, it must spend 4 pies of movement. Furthermore, the first time a creature enters the wall on a turn or ends its turn there, the creature must make a Dexterity tirada de salvacion. It recibe 7d8 slashing dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, both types of dano increase by 1d8 for each slot level above 6th.

Name: Wall of Water
Components: V, S, M (a drop of water)
Optional/Variant Classes: Druid (PHB'14), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create a wall of water on the ground at a point you can see within Alcance. You can make the wall up to 30 pies long, 10 pies high, and 1 foot thick, or you can make a ringed wall up to 20 pies in diameter, 20 pies high, and 1 foot thick. The wall vanishes when the spell ends. The wall's space is difficult terrain.

Any ataque de arma a distancia that enters the wall's space has disadvantage on the attack roll, and fire dano is halved if the fire effect passes through the wall to reach its target. Spells that deal cold dano that pass through the wall cause the area of the wall they pass through to freeze solid (at least a 5-foot-square section is frozen). Each 5-foot-square frozen section has AC 5 and 15 puntos de golpe. Reducing a frozen section to 0 puntos de golpe destroys it. When a section is destroyed, the wall's water doesn't fill it.

Name: Wardaway
Components: V, S, M (a miniature clay hand)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
You hurl a disorienting magical force toward una criatura within Alcance. el objetivo makes a Constitution tirada de salvacion; Constructs and No muerto automatically succeed on this save.

On a failed save, el objetivo recibe 2d4 Force dano, its Speed is halved until the start of your next turn, and on its next turn, it can take only an action or a Bonus Action (but not both). On a successful save, el objetivo recibe half as much dano only.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The dano increases by 2d4 for every spell slot level above 1.

Name: Warding Bond
Components: V, S, M (a pair of platinum rings worth at least 50 gp each, which you and el objetivo must wear for the Duracion)
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
This spell wards a willing creature you touch and creates a mystic connection between you and el objetivo until the spell ends. While el objetivo is within 60 pies of you, it gains a +1 bonus to AC and Tiradas de salvacion, and it has resistance to all dano. Also, each time it recibe dano, you take the same amount of dano.

The spell ends if you drop to 0 puntos de golpe or if you and el objetivo become separated by more than 60 pies. It also ends if the spell is cast again on either of the connected creatures. You can also dismiss the spell as an action.

Name: Warding Wind
Optional/Variant Classes: Bard (PHB'14), Bard (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'14), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A strong wind (20 miles per hour) blows around you in a 10-foot radius and moves with you, remaining centered on you. The wind lasts for the spell's Duracion.

The wind has the following effects:

It deafens you and other creatures in its area.It extinguishes unprotected flames in its area that are torch-sized or smaller.It hedges out vapor, gas, and fog that can be dispersed by strong wind.The area is difficult terrain for creatures other than you.The attack rolls of ranged arma attacks have disadvantage if the attacks pass in or out of the wind.

Name: Warp Sense
Classes: Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Warlock (PHB'14), Warlock (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
For the Duracion, you sense the presence of portals, even inactive ones, within 30 pies of yourself.

If you detect a portal in this way, you can use your action to study it. Make a DC 15 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a successful check, you learn the destination plane of the portal and what portal key it requires, then the spell ends. On a failed check, you learn nothing and can't study that portal again using this spell until you cast it again.

The spell can penetrate most barriers but is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of Comun metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 pies of wood or dirt.

Name: Water Breathing
Components: V, S, M (a short reed or piece of straw)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: This spell grants up to ten willing creatures you can see within Alcance the ability to breathe underwater until the spell ends. Affected creatures also retain their normal mode of respiration.

Name: Water Breathing
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: This spell grants up to ten willing creatures of your choice within Alcance the ability to breathe underwater until the spell ends. Affected creatures also retain their normal mode of respiration.

Name: Water Walk
Components: V, S, M (a piece of cork)
Subclasses: Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
This spell grants the ability to move across any liquid surface—such as water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, or lava—as if it were harmless solid ground (creatures crossing molten lava can still take dano from the heat). Up to ten willing creatures you can see within Alcance gain this ability for the Duracion.

If you target a creature submerged in a liquid, the spell carries el objetivo to the surface of the liquid at a rate of 60 pies per round.

Name: Water Walk
Components: V, S, M (a piece of cork)
Subclasses: Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:

Up to ten willing creatures of your choice within Alcance gain this ability for the Duracion.

An affected target must take a Bonus Action to pass from the liquid's surface into the liquid itself and vice versa, but if el objetivo falls into the liquid, el objetivo passes through the surface into the liquid below.

Name: Watery Sphere
Components: V, S, M (a droplet of water)
Optional/Variant Classes: Druid (PHB'14), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter
Text:
You conjure up a sphere of water with a 5-foot radius at a point you can see within Alcance. The sphere can hover but no more than 10 pies off the ground. The sphere remains for the spell's Duracion.

Any creature in the sphere's space must make a Strength tirada de salvacion. On a successful save, a creature is ejected from that space to the nearest unoccupied space of the creature's choice outside the sphere. A Enorme or larger creature succeeds on the tirada de salvacion automatically, and a Grande or smaller creature can choose to fail it. On a failed save, a creature is apresado by the sphere and is engulfed by the water. At the end of each of its turns, a apresado target can repeat the tirada de salvacion, ending the effect on itself on a success.

The sphere can restrain as many as four Mediano or smaller creatures or one Grande creature. If the sphere restrains a creature that causes it to exceed this capacity, a random creature that was already apresado by the sphere falls out of it and lands derribado in a space within 5 pies of it.

As an action, you can move the sphere up to 30 pies in a straight line. If it moves over a pit, a cliff, or other drop-off, it safely descends until it is hovering 10 pies above the ground. Any creature apresado by the sphere moves with it. You can ram the sphere into creatures, forcing them to make the tirada de salvacion.

When the spell ends, the sphere falls to the ground and extinguishes all normal flames within 30 pies of it. Any creature apresado by the sphere is knocked derribado in the space where it falls. The water then vanishes.

Name: Web
Components: V, S, M (a bit of spiderweb)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger
Text:
You conjure a mass of thick, sticky webbing at a point of your choice within Alcance. The webs fill a 20-foot cube from that point for the Duracion. The webs are difficult terrain and lightly obscure their area.

If the webs aren't anchored between two solid masses (such as walls or trees) or layered across a floor, wall, or ceiling, the conjured web collapses on itself, and the spell ends at the start of your next turn. Webs layered over a flat surface have a depth of 5 pies.

cada criatura that starts its turn in the webs or that enters them during its turn must make a Dexterity tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature is apresado as long as it remains in the webs or until it breaks free.

A creature apresado by the webs can use its action to make a Strength check against your CD de salvacion de conjuros. If it succeeds, it is no longer apresado.

The webs are flammable. Any 5-foot cube of webs exposed to fire burns away in 1 round, dealing 2d4 fire dano to any creature that starts its turn in the fire.

Name: Weird
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: Drawing on the deepest fears of a group of creatures, you create illusory creatures in their minds, visible only to them. cada criatura in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point of your choice within Alcance must make a Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a creature becomes asustado for the Duracion. The illusion calls on the creature's deepest fears, manifesting its worst nightmares as an implacable threat. At the end of each of the asustado creature's turns, it debe superar a Wisdom tirada de salvacion or take 4d10 psychic dano. On a successful save, the spell ends for that creature.

Name: Weird
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You try to create illusory terrors in others' minds. cada criatura of your choice in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within Alcance makes a Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a target recibe 10d10 Psychic dano and has the asustado condition for the Duracion. On a successful save, a target recibe half as much dano only.

A asustado target makes a Wisdom tirada de salvacion at the end of each of its turns. On a failed save, it recibe 5d10 Psychic dano. On a successful save, the spell ends on that target.

Name: Whirlwind
Optional/Variant Classes: Druid (PHB'14), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A whirlwind howls down to a point that you can see on the ground within Alcance. The whirlwind is a 10-foot-radius, 30-foot-high cylinder centered on that point. Until the spell ends, you can use your action to move the whirlwind up to 30 pies in any direction along the ground. The whirlwind sucks up any Mediano or smaller objects that aren't secured to anything and that aren't worn or carried by anyone.

A creature must make a Dexterity tirada de salvacion the first time on a turn that it enters the whirlwind or that the whirlwind enters its space, including when the whirlwind first appears. A creature recibe 10d6 bludgeoning dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. In addition, a Grande or smaller creature that fails the save debe superar a Strength tirada de salvacion or become apresado in the whirlwind until the spell ends. When a creature starts its turn apresado by the whirlwind, the creature is pulled 5 pies higher inside it, unless the creature is at the top. A apresado creature moves with the whirlwind and falls when the spell ends, unless the creature has some means to stay aloft.

A apresado creature can use an action to make a Strength or Dexterity check against your CD de salvacion de conjuros. If successful, the creature is no longer apresado by the whirlwind and is hurled 3d6 × 10 pies away from it in a random direction.

Name: Wind Walk
Components: V, S, M (fire and holy water)
Text:
You and up to ten willing creatures you can see within Alcance assume a gaseous form for the Duracion, appearing as wisps of cloud. While in this cloud form, a creature has a flying speed of 300 pies and has resistance to dano from nonmagical weapons. The only actions a creature can take in this form are the Dash action or to revert to its normal form. Reverting recibe 1 minute, during which time a creature is incapacitado and can't move. Until the spell ends, a creature can revert to cloud form, which also requires the 1-minute transformation.

If a creature is in cloud form and flying when the effect ends, the creature descends 60 pies per round for 1 minute until it lands, which it does safely. If it can't land after 1 minute, the creature falls the remaining distance.

Name: Wind Walk
Text:
You and up to ten willing creatures of your choice within Alcance assume gaseous forms for the Duracion, appearing as wisps of cloud. While in this cloud form, a target has a Volar Speed of 300 pies and can hover; it has Immunity to the derribado condition; and it has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing dano. The only actions a target can take in this form are the Dash action or a Magic action to begin reverting to its normal form. Reverting recibe 1 minute, during which el objetivo has the aturdido condition. Until the spell ends, el objetivo can revert to cloud form, which also requires a Magic action followed by a 1-minute transformation.

If a target is in cloud form and flying when the effect ends, el objetivo descends 60 pies per round for 1 minute until it lands, which it does safely. If it can't land after 1 minute, it falls the remaining distance.

Name: Wind Wall
Components: V, S, M (a Diminuto fan and a feather of exotic origin)
Subclasses: Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within Alcance. You can make the wall up to 50 pies long, 15 pies high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. The wall lasts for the Duracion.

When the wall appears, cada criatura within its area must make a Strength tirada de salvacion. A creature recibe 3d8 bludgeoning dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

The strong wind keeps fog, smoke, and other gases at bay. Pequeno or smaller flying creatures or objects can't pass through the wall. Loose, lightweight materials brought into the wall Volar upward. Arrows, bolts, and other ordinary projectiles launched at targets behind the wall are deflected upward and automatically miss. (Boulders hurled by giants or siege engines, and similar projectiles, are unaffected.) Creatures in gaseous form can't pass through it.

Name: Wind Wall
Components: V, S, M (a fan and a feather)
Subclasses: Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within Alcance. You can make the wall up to 50 pies long, 15 pies high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. The wall lasts for the Duracion.

When the wall appears, cada criatura in its area makes a Strength tirada de salvacion, taking 4d8 Bludgeoning dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

The strong wind keeps fog, smoke, and other gases at bay. Pequeno or smaller flying creatures or objects can't pass through the wall. Loose, lightweight materials brought into the wall Volar upward. Arrows, bolts, and other ordinary projectiles launched at targets behind the wall are deflected upward and miss automatically. Boulders hurled by Giants or siege engines, and similar projectiles, are unaffected. Creatures in gaseous form can't pass through it.

Name: Wish
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock
Text:
Wish is the mightiest spell a mortal creature can cast. By simply speaking aloud, you can alter the very foundations of reality in accord with your desires.

The basic use of this spell is to duplicate any other spell of 8th level or lower. You don't need to meet any requirements in that spell, including costly Componentes. The spell simply recibe effect. Alternatively, you can create one of the following effects of your choice:

You create one object of up to 25,000 gp in value that isn't a magic item. The object can be no more than 300 pies in any dimension, and it appears in an unoccupied space you can see on the ground.You allow up to twenty creatures that you can see to regain all puntos de golpe, and you end all effects on them described in the greater restoration spell.You grant up to ten creatures that you can see resistance to a dano type you choose.You grant up to ten creatures you can see immunity to a single spell or other magical effect for 8 hours. For instance, you could make yourself and all your companions immune to a lich's life drain attack.You undo a single recent event by forcing a reroll of any roll made within the last round (including your last turn). Reality reshapes itself to accommodate the new result. For example, a wish spell could undo an opponent's successful save, a foe's critical hit, or a friend's failed save. You can force the reroll to be made with advantage or disadvantage, and you can choose whether to use the reroll or the original roll.You might be able to achieve something beyond the scope of the above examples. State your wish to the DM as precisely as possible. The DM has great latitude in ruling what occurs in such an instance; the greater the wish, the greater the likelihood that something goes wrong. This spell might simply fail, the effect you desire might only be partly achieved, or you might suffer some unforeseen consequence as a result of how you worded the wish. For example, wishing that a villain were dead might propel you forward in time to a period when that villain is no longer alive, effectively removing you from the game. Similarly, wishing for a legendary magic item or artifact might instantly transport you to the presence of the item's current owner.

The stress of casting this spell to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell weakens you. After enduring that stress, each time you cast a spell until you finish a descanso largo, you take 1d10 necrotic dano per level of that spell. This dano can't be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, your Strength drops to 3, if it isn't 3 or lower already, for 2d4 days. For each of those days that you spend resting and doing nothing more than light activity, your remaining recovery time decreases by 2 days. Finally, there is a 33 percent chance that you are unable to cast wish ever again if you suffer this stress.

Name: Wish
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock
Text:
Wish is the mightiest spell a mortal can cast. By simply speaking aloud, you can alter reality itself.

The basic use of this spell is to duplicate any other spell of level 8 or lower. If you use it this way, you don't need to meet any requirements to cast that spell, including costly Componentes. The spell simply recibe effect.

Alternatively, you can create one of the following effects of your choice:

Object Creation. You create one object of up to 25,000 GP in value that isn't a magic item. The object can be no more than 300 pies in any dimension, and it appears in an unoccupied space that you can see on the ground.

Instant Health. You allow yourself and up to twenty creatures that you can see to regain all puntos de golpe, and you end all effects on them listed in the Greater Restoration spell.

Resistance. You grant up to ten creatures that you can see Resistance to one dano type that you choose. This Resistance is permanent.

Spell Immunity. You grant up to ten creatures you can see immunity to a single spell or other magical effect for 8 hours.

Sudden Learning. You replace one of your feats with another feat for which you are eligible. You lose all the benefits of the old feat and gain the benefits of the new one. You can't replace a feat that is a prerequisite for any of your other feats or features.

Roll Redo. You undo a single recent event by forcing a reroll of any die roll made within the last round (including your last turn). Reality reshapes itself to accommodate the new result. For example, a Wish spell could undo an ally's failed tirada de salvacion or a foe's Critical Hit. You can force the reroll to be made with Advantage or Disadvantage, and you choose whether to use the reroll or the original roll.

Reshape Reality. You may wish for something not included in any of the other effects. To do so, state your wish to the DM as precisely as possible. The DM has great latitude in ruling what occurs in such an instance; the greater the wish, the greater the likelihood that something goes wrong. This spell might simply fail, the effect you desire might be achieved only in part, or you might suffer an unforeseen consequence as a result of how you worded the wish. For example, wishing that a villain were dead might propel you forward in time to a period when that villain is no longer alive, effectively removing you from the game. Similarly, wishing for a Legendary magic item or an Artifact might instantly transport you to the presence of the item's current owner. If your wish is granted and its effects have consequences for a whole community, region, or world, you are likely to attract powerful foes. If your wish would affect a god, the god's divine servants might instantly intervene to prevent it or to encourage you to craft the wish in a particular way. If your wish would undo the multiverse itself, threaten the City of Sigil, or affect the Lady of Pain in any way, you see an image of her in your mind for a moment; she shakes her head, and your wish fails.

The stress of casting Wish to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell weakens you. After enduring that stress, each time you cast a spell until you finish a descanso largo, you take 1d10 Necrotic dano per level of that spell. This dano can't be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, your Strength score becomes 3 for 2d4 days. For each of those days that you spend resting and doing nothing more than light activity, your remaining recovery time decreases by 2 days. Finally, there is a 33 percent chance that you are unable to cast Wish ever again if you suffer this stress.

Name: Witch Bolt
Components: V, S, M (a twig from a tree that has been struck by lightning)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: A beam of crackling, blue energy lances out toward a creature within Alcance, forming a sustained arc of lightning between you and el objetivo. Make a ranged spell attack against that creature. On a hit, el objetivo recibe 1d12 lightning dano, and on each of your turns for the Duracion, you can use your action to deal 1d12 lightning dano to el objetivo automatically. The spell ends if you use your action to do anything else. The spell also ends if el objetivo is ever outside the spell's Alcance or if it has total cover from you.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the initial dano increases by 1d12 for each slot level above 1st.

Name: Witch Bolt
Components: V, S, M (a twig struck by lightning)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A beam of crackling energy lances toward a creature within Alcance, forming a sustained arc of lightning between you and el objetivo. Make a ranged spell attack against it. On a hit, el objetivo recibe 2d12 Lightning dano.

On each of your subsequent turns, you can take a Bonus Action to deal 1d12 Lightning dano to el objetivo automatically, even if the first attack missed.

The spell ends if el objetivo is ever outside the spell's Alcance or if it has Total Cover from you.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The initial dano increases by 1d12 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Wither and Bloom
Components: V, S, M (a withered vine twisted into a loop)
Classes: Druid (PHB'14), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'14), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'14), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
You invoke both death and life upon a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within Alcance. cada criatura of your choice in that area must make a Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 2d6 necrotic dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Nonmagical vegetation in that area withers.

In addition, una criatura of your choice in that area can spend and roll one of its unspent Hit Dice and regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to the roll plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
At Higher Levels: A niveles superiores. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the dano increases by 1d6 for each slot above the 2nd, and the number of Hit Dice that can be spent and added to the healing roll increases by one for each slot above 2nd.

Name: Word of Radiance
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You utter a divine word, and burning radiance erupts from you. cada criatura of your choice that you can see within Alcance debe superar a Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 1d6 radiant dano.

The spell's dano increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

Name: Word of Radiance
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: Burning radiance erupts from you in a 5-foot Emanation. cada criatura of your choice that you can see in it debe superar a Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 1d6 Radiant dano.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The dano increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Word of Recall
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You and up to five willing creatures within 5 pies of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. You and any creatures that teleport with you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to the spot you designated when you prepared your sanctuary (see below). If you cast this spell without first preparing a sanctuary, the spell has no effect.

You must designate a sanctuary by casting this spell within a location, such as a temple, dedicated to or strongly linked to your deity. If you attempt to cast the spell in this manner in an area that isn't dedicated to your deity, the spell has no effect.

Name: Word of Recall
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You and up to five willing creatures within 5 pies of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. You and any creatures that teleport with you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to the spot you designated when you prepared your sanctuary (see below). If you cast this spell without first preparing a sanctuary, the spell has no effect.

You must designate a location, such as a temple, as a sanctuary by casting this spell there.

Name: Wrath of Nature
Text:
You call out to the spirits of nature to rouse them against your enemies. Choose a point you can see within Alcance. The spirits cause trees, rocks, and grasses in a 60-foot cube centered on that point to become animated until the spell ends.

Grasses and Undergrowth. Any area of ground in the cube that is covered by grass or undergrowth is difficult terrain for your enemies.

Trees. At the start of each of your turns, each of your enemies within 10 pies of any tree in the cube debe superar a Dexterity tirada de salvacion or take 4d6 slashing dano from whipping branches.

Roots and Vines. At the end of each of your turns, una criatura of your choice that is on the ground in the cube debe superar a Strength tirada de salvacion or become apresado until the spell ends. A apresado creature can use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your CD de salvacion de conjuros, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Rocks. As a bonus action on your turn, you can cause a loose rock in the cube to launch at a creature you can see in the cube. Make a ranged spell attack against el objetivo. On a hit, el objetivo recibe 3d8 nonmagical bludgeoning dano, and it debe superar a Strength tirada de salvacion or fall derribado.

Name: Wrathful Smite
Subclasses: Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: The next time you hit with a ataque de arma cuerpo a cuerpo during this spell's Duracion, your attack deals an extra 1d6 psychic dano. Additionally, if el objetivo is a creature, it must make a Wisdom tirada de salvacion o quedar asustado of you until the spell ends. As an action, the creature can make a Wisdom check against your CD de salvacion de conjuros to steel its resolve and end this spell.

Name: Wrathful Smite
Subclasses: Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: el objetivo recibe an extra 1d6 Necrotic dano from the attack, and it debe superar a Wisdom tirada de salvacion or have the asustado condition until the spell ends. At the end of each of its turns, the asustado target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The dano increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Wristpocket
Subclasses: Chronurgy (EGW) Wizard, Chronurgy (EGW) Wizard, Graviturgy (EGW) Wizard, Graviturgy (EGW) Wizard
Text:
You flick your wrist, causing one object in your hand to vanish. The object, which only you can be holding and can weigh no more than 5 pounds, is transported to an extradimensional space, where it remains for the Duracion.

Until the spell ends, you can use your action to summon the object to your free hand, and you can use your action to return the object to the extradimensional space. An object still in the pocket plane when the spell ends appears in your space, at your pies.

Name: Yolande's Regal Presence
Subclasses: Glory (PHB'24) Paladin
Text: You surround yourself with unearthly majesty in a 10-foot Emanation. Whenever the Emanation enters the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, el objetivo recibe 4d6 Psychic dano and has the derribado condition, and you can push it up to 10 pies away. On a successful save, el objetivo recibe half as much dano only. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

Name: Zephyr Strike
Text:
You move like the wind. Until the spell ends, your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

Once before the spell ends, you can give yourself advantage on one arma attack roll on your turn. That attack deals an extra 1d8 force dano on a hit. Whether you hit or miss, your walking speed increases by 30 pies hasta el final de that turn.

Name: Zone of Truth
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You create a magical zone that guards against deception in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on a point of your choice within Alcance. Until the spell ends, a creature that enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must make a Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a creature can't speak a deliberate lie while in the radius. You know whether cada criatura succeeds or fails on its tirada de salvacion.

An affected creature is aware of the spell and can thus avoid answering questions to which it would normally respond with a lie. Such creatures can be evasive in its answers as long as it remains within the boundaries of the truth.

Name: Zone of Truth
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You create a magical zone that guards against deception in a 15-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within Alcance. Until the spell ends, a creature that enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there makes a Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a creature can't speak a deliberate lie while in the radius. You know whether a creature succeeds or fails on this save.

An affected creature is aware of the spell and can avoid answering questions to which it would normally respond with a lie. Such a creature can be evasive yet must be truthful.
